comments.text: GPT-5.5 Pro claims a solution to the 2nd question here.

Genuine question, the rate at which you and some others here have been posting has been borderline crazy, in a good way. IS there a certain technique you use for scouting problems? In the codex app, I let around 12 subagents scout out problems in depth then determine on a ranking scale which is quickest for a solve, and as of now, I have gotten maybe one solve on 689, still hasn't been reviewed yet. I was wondering if there was any advice you could give here, and also an interesting solution from 5.5, are there prompting techniques you give or are these natural? Anyways, amazing work and best of luck with future problems.

Congrats. Standard check found no issues. However, the proof seems very short and the problem (second question) could be reasonably well known e.g. it is mentioned in this paper (Introduction, near the end of paragraph 7). I would appreciate further scrutinizing.
